viewsContainer.notes.title: GroveNotes
view.notes.name: GroveNotes
viewsWelcome.notesView.contents:
$(notebook) Notes

No notes folder configured yet.
Select a folder to start managing your Markdown notes.

[$(folder-opened) Select Notes Folder](command:grove-notes.selectNotesFolder)

Or run `Notes: Setup Notes Folder` from the Command Palette (Ctrl+Shift+P).

Already have a path? $(settings-gear) [Open Settings](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%5B%22grove-notes.notePath%22%5D) to set it directly.

Other settings include templates path, default note title format, ignore patterns, tags, and Jujutsu version control options. Explore the settings to customize your note-taking experience!

$(settings-gear) [Open Extension Settings](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%5B%22grove-notes%22%5D)
config.title: Notes
config.templatePath.description: Path to the templates folder. Defaults to <notePath>/.templates if empty.
config.defaultNoteTitle.description: File name format for new notes. Supports {{YYYY}}, {{MM}}, {{DD}}, {{HH}}, {{mm}}, {{ss}}, {{title}}.
config.defaultExtension.description: Default file extension for new notes
config.view.hiddenPatterns.description: Folder/file name patterns to hide from the tree view only. These folders are still scanned for tags and recent notes. Supports glob patterns (e.g. `_*`, `*.draft`).
config.view.useGitignore.description:
When enabled, patterns from the `.gitignore` file in the notes folder are applied to all scans (tags, recent notes, tree view). `.git` and `.jj` are always excluded regardless of this setting.

Negation patterns (`!`) are ignored. Path-based patterns such as `src/**/*.tmp` are supported.

[Open .gitignore](command:grove-notes.openGitignore)
config.counterStartsAtOne.description: When true, duplicate file counters start at 1 (e.g. {{N}}→_1, _2… or name_1, _2…). When false (default), the first file has no counter and conflicts begin at _2.
config.tags.predefined.description: Predefined tag list shown in the Edit Tags quick picker. Tags not in this list can still be typed manually.
config.tags.scanWorkspace.description: When enabled, Edit Tags also scans all notes in the workspace and adds any discovered tags to the picker candidates. Disable for large note collections to avoid performance impact.
config.preview.openToSide.description:
When enabled, the Markdown preview opens to the side (split view). When disabled, it opens in the current editor column.

Note: VS Code's built-in Markdown preview reuses a single tab per editor group regardless of this setting. To keep multiple previews open simultaneously, pin the preview tab (right-click the tab → Pin).
config.versionControl.autoCommitInterval: Auto-commit interval in minutes using Jujutsu. Set to 0 to disable.
Commit message for auto-commits. Supports date tokens.
config.versionControl.useJujutsu.descrip:
Enable Jujutsu (jj) version control features. When false, all jj commands and toolbar buttons are hidden.

Jujutsu is a Git-compatible version control system. [Learn more](https://www.jj-vcs.dev/latest/) about Jujutsu.
config.versionControl.jjInitMode.descrip: Controls whether jj is automatically initialized when a notes folder is selected and no .jj repository exists.
config.versionControl.jjInitMode.always: Always initialize without asking, whether or not a .git repository exists.
config.versionControl.jjInitMode.ask: Always show a confirmation dialog before initializing.
config.versionControl.jjInitMode.askIfGi: Show a confirmation dialog only when a .git repository is detected. Skip otherwise.
config.versionControl.jjInitMode.autoAsk: Initialize automatically when no .git exists. Show a confirmation dialog when .git is detected.
